scope: Source-backed distillation techniques and engineering lessons relevant to TSQBEV's teacher/student regime.
source_policy: Primary sources only: official paper pages and official code repositories when available.
design_laws: Distill where the student is weak, not where the teacher is merely expressive.
Teacher-free and peer-based distillation help only when optimization noise is the main problem; they do not replace missing geometry or world supervision.
Output and quality-map distillation are usually higher ROI than architecture-specific hidden-state copying when the student family is different from the teacher.

entries.title: Be Your Own Teacher
entries.core_trick: Use deeper or later representations of the same network to teach shallower or earlier submodules during training.
entries.intuition: Self-distillation is a regularizer that stabilizes optimization and representation learning. It helps when the model already knows what to learn but not how to organize it efficiently.
ablation_takeaways: Self-distillation improves generalization when the student architecture is already reasonable.
It is not the right first move when the missing signal is external geometry or multimodal teacher knowledge.
ablation_nuggets: Apply it after the main teacher supervision path is working, not before.
Use it to stabilize the student head or intermediate world features, not as a substitute for teacher BEV distillation.
apply_when: the student is close to working but overfits or optimizes noisily
you want a cheap extra regularizer without another external teacher
avoid_when: the model lacks strong external supervision or world grounding
the student is underpowered and already capacity-limited
entries.integration_pattern: Use on the student's own multiscale or late-vs-early features after external teacher supervision is stable.
failure_modes: wasting budget on self-regularization before fixing the actual supervision bottleneck
copying poor late predictions back into earlier features
tsqbev_actions: defer until DINOv3 plus teacher BEV/quality supervision is stable
apply to student head refinement only if the external teacher path plateaus for optimization reasons

entries.title: Deep Mutual Learning
entries.core_trick: Train multiple students together and let them align predictions so each student benefits from peer regularization.
entries.intuition: Peer learning helps when multiple students see the same task from slightly different inductive biases or augmentations. It is weaker when everyone shares the same blind spot.
ablation_takeaways: Mutual learning can outperform static distillation when peers are diverse enough.
If all peers are weak in the same way, they amplify each other's mistakes instead of fixing them.
ablation_nuggets: Use only when there is real architectural or view diversity across students.
Do not replace a strong external teacher with peer alignment if the students are all small and underinformed.
apply_when: you can afford at least two diverse student views or architectures during training
you want extra regularization after a strong external teacher baseline exists
avoid_when: all students are nearly identical and make the same errors
GPU budget is already dominated by the real teacher path
entries.integration_pattern: Use as a later-stage regularizer across small student variants or temporal/view-specific student heads.
failure_modes: peer collapse around the same bad mode
extra compute with no information gain over a better single-teacher setup
tsqbev_actions: defer until one strong single-student recipe exists
if used, diversify the peers by backbone or modality emphasis rather than duplicating the same small model

entries.title: Born-Again Networks
entries.core_trick: Iteratively distill a model into another model of the same architecture and use the previous generation as the teacher.
entries.intuition: BAN works when the optimization landscape itself is the issue and a better teacher trajectory can improve the same student family.
ablation_takeaways: Iterative teacher refresh can help even without bigger architectures.
It is still secondary to getting the teacher signal and task representation right in the first place.
ablation_nuggets: Treat BAN as a later exploit, not an architectural reset.
Use only after official metrics are already nontrivial and stable.
apply_when: a stable recipe exists and you want incremental generalization gains
teacher refresh is cheaper than collecting stronger supervision
avoid_when: the current teacher or student family is fundamentally wrong
the export path is still unstable or zeroed out
entries.integration_pattern: Use on the winning student family after the external teacher regime is settled.
failure_modes: compounding the same architectural mistake across generations
spending training budget on iteration before addressing core bottlenecks
tsqbev_actions: use only after the frontier student beats the current v28 control
treat as exploit-stage refinement, never as the first major fix

entries.title: Quality-Map Distillation
entries.core_trick: Distill dense quality-aware class or occupancy maps instead of only box lists so the student inherits ranking information and spatial confidence structure.
entries.intuition: Our current bottleneck is not just object presence. It is object ranking and geometric plausibility. Dense quality targets teach the student where high-confidence world structure should live.
ablation_takeaways: Quality-aware output targets usually beat plain focal hard-negative tuning when overproduction and ranking are the real problem.
Dense output supervision is often cheaper and more architecture-agnostic than hidden-state matching.
ablation_nuggets: Prioritize this before self-distillation or mutual learning.
Use when the teacher is much stronger and already produces reliable scores or region maps.
apply_when: teacher outputs are trustworthy but student ranking/export is weak
the student family differs too much from the teacher for direct feature copying
avoid_when: teacher outputs are noisy or geometry is inconsistent
the student has no spatial target interface at all
entries.integration_pattern: Distill teacher quality maps or region priors into student world tokens, BEV cells, or region-objectness heads.
failure_modes: teacher noise being baked into a dense target
tsqbev_actions: keep prioritizing teacher quality and region priors over more sparse query heuristics
use dense teacher supervision on the new DINOv3 branch before trying student-only regularizers
